prompt.text:
You are running the La Lluvia phase of a Holmes case.
FIXTURE MATERIALS (the only facts in evidence):
E1: an objection letter from an adjacent property owner, dated 2025-11-04, is in the permit docket [fixture/permit-docket.md section 3].
E2: a clerk's-office bulletin announces a processing backlog covering March through June 2026 [fixture/clerk-bulletin.md section 2].
E3: the enforcement log shows no violation or enforcement action against the parcel [fixture/enforcement-log.md section 1].
The permit application itself was filed 2025-10-01 and last updated 2025-11-06 [fixture/permit-docket.md section 1].
CASE QUESTION: why has the fixture community-garden permit (docket 441) stalled for nine months?
Generate at least three distinct candidate hypotheses. Do not rank or discard any. Make no factual claims beyond the materials. Reply with one line per hypothesis, exactly in this format and nothing else:
H: <statement> | PRESENT: <evidence you would expect to find if true> | ABSENT: <evidence you would expect to be absent if true>

content.text:
(no output)

Command exited with code 1

update.title: reading permit docket clerk bulletin enforcement log

rawInput.content:
- [x] Review fixture materials (E1, E2, E3, application dates) as given in prompt (files not accessible on disk, used inline facts)
- [ ] Generate >=3 distinct hypotheses explaining 9-month stall of docket 441
- [ ] For each: state PRESENT (expected evidence if true) and ABSENT (expected absent evidence if true)
- [ ] No ranking, no discarding, no facts beyond fixture materials
- [ ] Output strictly in required one-line-per-hypothesis format, nothing else

content.text: Updated (460 chars)

update.title: updating todo list with docket analysis tasks

content.text: H: The adjacent-
owner objection (E1) triggered a review or dispute-resolution process that has kept docket 441 pending. | PRESENT: doc
ket entries scheduling a hearing or requesting applicant response to the objection, correspondence addressing the objector's concerns. | ABSENT: en
forcement or violation action against the parcel (consistent with E3), a docket entry recording the

objection as resolved or withdrawn.
H: The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall. | PRESEN
T: docket entries placing the application in a queue during March-June 2026, staff correspondence cit
ing the backlog bulletin. | ABSENT: docket activity or status change between 2025-11-06 and the start

of the backlog in March 2026.
H: The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, st
alling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's. | PRESENT
: a request-for-information or incomplete-application notice dated after 2025-11-06, no further applicant submissions in
the docket. | ABSENT: any enforcement-log entry against the parcel (consistent with E3), any docket reference to the backlog (

E2) as the cause.
H: The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i
.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months pre
ceding the backlog. | PRESENT: a gap in docket entries between 2025-11-06 and the backlog period with no ass
igned staff action noted. | ABSENT: any hearing, request, objection response
, or enforcement entry (per E3) logged for docket 441 during that gap.

prompt.text:
Same case. Hypotheses:
H1: The adjacent-owner objection (E1) triggered a review or dispute-resolution process that has kept docket 441 pending.
H2: The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall.
H3: The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, stalling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's.
H4: The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months preceding the backlog.
Considering ONLY evidence E1 (the 2025-11-04 objection letter): estimate the probability of observing E1 if H1 is true, and if H1 is false (some other hypothesis true). Values in (0,1], two decimals. Reply exactly one line:
LR: P_IF_TRUE=<value> P_IF_FALSE=<value>

content.text: L
R: P_IF_TRUE=0.90 P_IF_FALSE=0.40

prompt.text:
Same case. Hypotheses:
H1: The adjacent-owner objection (E1) triggered a review or dispute-resolution process that has kept docket 441 pending.
H2: The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall.
H3: The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, stalling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's.
H4: The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months preceding the backlog.
Evidence: E1 (objection letter), E2 (backlog bulletin), E3 (no enforcement action). Score every hypothesis-evidence cell as C (consistent), I (inconsistent), or NA (not applicable), seeking disconfirmation. Reply with exactly one line per cell, format:
ACH: H<number> E<number> <C|I|NA>

prompt.text:
You are running the El Diablo pass (devil's advocate) of the same case.
Hypotheses:
H1: The adjacent-owner objection (E1) triggered a review or dispute-resolution process that has kept docket 441 pending.
H2: The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall.
H3: The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, stalling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's.
H4: The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months preceding the backlog.
Attack the CURRENTLY LEADING hypothesis (assume it is H1) as hard as the fixture materials allow. Do not invent facts. Reply in exactly two lines:
CHALLENGE: <your strongest contrary argument>
WOULD-DAMAGE: <the single piece of evidence that would most damage H1 if it existed>

content.text: CHALLENGE: The docket's last update is 2025-11-06, only
two days after the objection letter (E1) was filed — if E1 had truly launched an active
review or dispute-resolution track, one would expect continuing docket activity (hearing notices, applicant responses, sta
ff correspondence) across the following nine months, but no such subsequent entries are in evidence; the record
shows only silence after that single update, which is equally consistent with the file simply going untouched (H4) r

ather than being actively held up by the objection.
WOULD-DAMAGE: A docket ent
ry showing the objection (E1) was logged and closed/administratively noted on or
shortly after 2025-11-06 with no further review action opened — this would show E1 was received and sh
elved rather than having triggered any ongoing process.

evidence.description: objection letter dated 2025-11-04 in the docket
no enforcement action on the parcel (absence evidence)

following_the_money: not applicable: the fixture names no financial actor or flow; recorded explicitly per the phase rule

The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall.
The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, stalling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's.
The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months preceding the backlog.
la_lluvia.raw:
H: The adjacent-owner objection (E1) triggered a review or dispute-resolution process that has kept docket 441 pending. | PRESENT: docket entries scheduling a hearing or requesting applicant response to the objection, correspondence addressing the objector's concerns. | ABSENT: enforcement or violation action against the parcel (consistent with E3), a docket entry recording the objection as resolved or withdrawn.
H: The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall. | PRESENT: docket entries placing the application in a queue during March-June 2026, staff correspondence citing the backlog bulletin. | ABSENT: docket activity or status change between 2025-11-06 and the start of the backlog in March 2026.
H: The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, stalling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's. | PRESENT: a request-for-information or incomplete-application notice dated after 2025-11-06, no further applicant submissions in the docket. | ABSENT: any enforcement-log entry against the parcel (consistent with E3), any docket reference to the backlog (E2) as the cause.
H: The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months preceding the backlog. | PRESENT: a gap in docket entries between 2025-11-06 and the backlog period with no assigned staff action noted. | ABSENT: any hearing, request, objection response, or enforcement entry (per E3) logged for docket 441 during that gap.

ach_ranking.hypothesis: The adjacent-owner objection (E1) triggered a review or dispute-resolution process that has kept docket 441 pending.
The clerk's-office processing backlog (E2) is directly responsible for the stall.
The applicant failed to submit additional information requested after the 2025-11-06 update, stalling the file on the applicant's side rather than the office's.
The file was administratively set aside with no formal action recorded, i.e. it fell through normal processing without a documented reason during the months preceding the backlog.

the_wall.diablo_raw:
CHALLENGE: The docket's last update is 2025-11-06, only two days after the objection letter (E1) was filed — if E1 had truly launched an active review or dispute-resolution track, one would expect continuing docket activity (hearing notices, applicant responses, staff correspondence) across the following nine months, but no such subsequent entries are in evidence; the record shows only silence after that single update, which is equally consistent with the file simply going untouched (H4) rather than being actively held up by the objection.
WOULD-DAMAGE: A docket entry showing the objection (E1) was logged and closed/administratively noted on or shortly after 2025-11-06 with no further review action opened — this would show E1 was received and shelved rather than having triggered any ongoing process.

kac.statement: the fixture docket is the complete record

question: why has the fixture community-garden permit (docket 441) stalled for nine months?
